what:
OpenFn adaptor for Odoo (ERP). The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Odoo ERP.

It exposes 6 operations that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. 5 ready-to-paste snippets are grouped as create (3) and search (2). Vendor homepage: https://www.odoo.com.
connect:
Odoo supports HTTP Basic auth for API access.

The credential schema asks for baseUrl (required), username (required), password (required), and database (required). Auth reference: https://www.odoo.com/documentation/master/developer/reference/external_api.html.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Odoo ERP.

2 triggers are catalogued (0 webhook, 2 polling), including New Contact, New Lead. See the Triggers tab for the full list. Odoo doesn't appear to expose webhooks, so plan on polling at a sensible interval.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (polled on a cron). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Odoo. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Odoo jobs at volume:

- Odoo doesn't surface webhooks, so poll on the longest interval your SLA allows and use a since-cursor to avoid re-reading everything.
- Batch requests where the API supports it and avoid fan-out loops over large collections.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Odoo exposes XML-RPC and JSON-RPC; schemas come from model introspection (ir.model / fields_get), not a static spec. apiStyle=jsonrpc extends the default enum.
best:
- Store Odoo credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-odoo to v2.1.11 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
